entry.title: Re: ABAP Message Channel Bindings
entry.content: Thank you!

entry.title: 🇳🇱 SAP CodeJam: Service Integration with CAP ('s-Hertogenbosch, NL)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam introduces attendees to service integration with the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP). Specifically, the Node.js flavor of CAP is used.

During this event, you'll become acquainted with how external services can be integrated and consumed from within a CAP project. In working through the exercises you'll also become comfortable with various facilities that CDS offers for declaring, relating and exposing entities, and familiar with how the SAP Cloud SDK covers connectivity and destinations, supporting such integration scenarios with CAP.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Fri 23 May 2025 in 's-Hertogenbosch, NL. It is being kindly hosted by the lovely folks at newFounders - the CodeJam will take place in their offices. Thanks! 

The instructor is Developer Advocate DJ Adams. 

There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: https://github.com/SAP-samples/cap-service-integration-codejam/blob/main/prerequisites.md.

If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "I'm attending" facility on this page. Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees for this event. In that context, if you indicate that you're attending and later realise you can't, please remove your name so that others get a chance instead. Thanks!

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Re: CPI Manage Security Material OAuth2 Client Credential...
entry.content:
Hello,

so on premise call to a token provider is not directly possible via Security Material.

https://me.sap.com/notes/3474373/E

entry.title: Re: LMS Search for Library-No libraries were found with t...
entry.content: Is there another Learning admin that has the same role and is this Learning admin also impacted? Try changing the role to "ALL" to verify the system behavior.

entry.title: Do you know how to manage your transportation processes effectively?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Exploring Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Transportation Management learning journey.

Overview
This course will help you to prepare for the blueprinting stage of your SAP Transportation Management implementation or enable you to use the system in your role as a business user.

Learning objectives 
You will gain an overview of the key business transactions and system capabilities to enable you to design the processes you will use within your own organization. You will learn how SAP TM can help you to support Order Management, Transportation Planning, Subcontracting, Execution, Charge Calculation and Settlement.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: How to Create a Section for Work Center Views in the UI Designer
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

Does anyone know how to create a section group for work center views in UI Designer? The sample image below is divided into 3 sections. I want to do the same with my custom business objects. I also want to know how to include "Common Task" on the work center.

entry.title: Criticality in Projection View of RAP BO with draft, best practice?
entry.content:
Hi RAP developers,

in our draft enabled RAP BO (for business configuration) we have a Lifecyle State field (DRAFT, RELEASED, DEPRECATED). We want to show Criticality Colors (ie @ui.identification.criticality, @ui.lineitem.criticality), Criticality needs to be 1,3,2 respectivly.

So where to calculate this, ie do

case LifecycleState when 'DEPRECATED' then 2 when 'DRAFT' then 1 else 3 end as LifecycleCriticality

?

In the Projection View I get "Field LIFECYCLECRITICALITY contains a not supported expression".

When I do this in the R View, IMHO I have to do this also in the Query View for the draft, or? This would not so nice, having to write this twice...

Why can't I add such simple calculations which are UI related in the C View (and not have this field in an API I-View)?

Any other suggestions? What is best practice here?

I am aware of https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/api/sap.ui.comp.smartfield.SmartField%23annotations/IsConfigurationDeprecationCode but IMHO this is not supported in RAP...

Regards,

  Wolfgang

entry.title: Re: DWS class grouping
entry.content: You use it to create evaluation criteria for a daily work schedule. According to the evaluation criteria, daily work schedules can be used in differen ways in time wage type selection, attendance and absence counts, or in time evaluation. The working times defined in a daily work schedule are not influenced by the assignment of a daily work schedule class. As a result, the daily work schedule class respresents an additional evaluation of a daily work schedule. Daily work schedule classes can be identified by any number between 0 and 9. Example: You want to specify a working time model in which Saturday is a day off but your employees can work if they choose to do so. You define a daily work schedule with the desired planned hours and classify it with the daily work schedule class 0, which is stored as a day off.

entry.title: Re: SAP Data Services Designer crash after adding functio...
entry.content: I've solved it, the problem was the selection of output parameter (I had to select AL_RFC_RETCODE). Thank you.

entry.title: Can someone help me on how to activate batch strategy on storage location
entry.content: SD (Sales and Distribution)

entry.title: cwip asset number and fixed asset number, difference? pathway to WBS elements via tables and fields
entry.content:
Hi all,
I am making a Z report. It should display several object fields, including CWIP asset number and Fixed asset number for the corresponding WBS element. For this, as I found, this should be linked to wbs element via tables and common fields.

so, how to differentiate in the report or create a logical pathway for the two when both CWIP asset number and Fixed asset number has same tables and fields i.e. ANLA and ANLN1?

entry.title: After running the script recorded in SAP, the system closes and errors apear
Re: Important Update: Changes to the Free Tier Option for SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content:
Hi All,

During my ABAP Cloud development in the Free Tier (not the Trial Account) version, I noticed that some standard function modules and classes that I frequently use in my S/4HANA system are not accessible.

Are these standard objects restricted in the Free Tier version?
Will I gain access to them if I switch to the Pay-As-You-Go plan?

If anyone has any knowledge about this, kindly share your insights.

Thanks!

@FrankJentsch @Burcu_Karlidag

entry.title: Re: Can assign users to specific user group in target sys...
entry.content:
No, as per SAP Note 2984603, SAP Identity Provisioning Service (IPS) does not natively support assigning users to specific user groups in SAP AS ABAP on-premise. The note specifically lists supported target systems, including SAP Ariba, SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Commerce Cloud, SAP SuccessFactors, and others that use SCIM or other APIs for user provisioning.

For SAP AS ABAP on-premise, you would typically need to use an indirect approach, such as:

  1. Using SAP Identity Management (IdM): If integrated with IPS, IdM can assign roles and groups in ABAP systems.
  2. Custom Provisioning via BAdIs: Implementing BAdIs (e.g., BADI_IDENTITY_PROVISIONING) in the ABAP system to process user assignments.
  3. ABAP Custom Reports or Scripts: Extracting user assignments from IPS and applying them using ABAP reports (e.g., SU01, PFCG role assignments).
  4. LDAP Integration: If your ABAP system supports LDAP authentication, IPS could provision users to an LDAP directory that the ABAP system queries.

entry.title: Re: vf01 negative balance error and user exit with export parameter = import parameters
entry.content: Basically, since you didn't deactivate the CMOD projects, the exit FMs are executed and if SAP didn't prefill the returned/changed parameters, they are sent back cleared, initial.

entry.title: Re: Is there a hard limit on the number bookmarks in SAP...
entry.content:
@PatriciaR55 

I am not aware of a hard limit in terms of count but there is a limit on size of bookmarks. As per the note below its 90 MB.

3574030 - What is the size limit of saved bookmarks for stories in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) ? - SAP for Me

 

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: How to include Project System in Make-to-Stock proces...
entry.content:
Project system is a module and Make-To-Stock is a Business process. Probably, you need to give a brief explanation on

  • what the Business process is,
  • what efforts you made so far to configure and integrate with PS
  • if system gives any error during your configuration, share the screen shot

Gopi

Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to regenerate the system certificates in sap po 7.5?
entry.content:
Dear Gopi, Greetings

In order to update/regenerate the system certificates for your PO systems please refer to the bellow SAP KBA and follow the steps provided in the resolution section starting from the element 2/:

1770585 - How to configure SSL on the AS Java

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: How to generate your SAP Datasphere documentation aut...
entry.content:
There is no need to code, just a few formulas in the views in SAP Datasphere.
The model thus created allows you to directly do the documentation in realtime as in the example.


The objective of the post is to show through the lineage the sources that allow you to do what is necessary.

My current problem is to find the metadata data such as the Business Purpose, the AM formulas as well as the labels because only the technical names are available

entry.title: Difference between document currency amount and hard currency amount in FBL3N
entry.content:
Hello all,

User have posted some documents in which the document currency and Hard currency ( Local currency 2 ) is the same but in FBL3N report it  is showing two different amounts for the same currency.

For example

Below amounts we can see in the FBL3N report
Document currency is MZN - 1,669,042.28

Hard currency ( Local currency 2 ) is also MZN  - 1,673,836.60

Can anyone help on how can there be a difference between document currency and local currency 2 when its the same currency having same exchange rate ?

 

Regards

entry.content:
While submitting electronic invoicing for Brazil, I am getting the below errors. although I maintained all the required fields still I am getting the same error message and not allowing to submit the billing document.

I checked master data and In change nota fiscal I maintained "Financial Data" and "NF-e" tabs but still i am getting the below errors. Can you please suggest what else i can check.

entry.title: Re: How To Create A Basic Custom Entity?
entry.content:
I don't agree

Why Use Custom Entities?

  • No Database Needed: You don't need to create database tables. not "No database needed", if you have data in db that you can use, why bother to use custom entity?
  • Flexible Data Sources: Fetch data from APIs, services, or other systems.
  • Great for Reporting: Use it to display data without saving anything. great for reporting are you kidding?
  • Works with CDS Views: You can combine it with other SAP data models.

custom entity just gives you an option to model your data model where your data cannot be fetched from db directly. nothing great.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting - New Balance Validation Process
entry.content:
@harivenkatesh_subramanian 

First, I would like to convey my regards and appreciation for sharing this valuable information. It has been truly helpful.

I have a few clarifications that I raised with SAP Support regarding the new features and the Balance Validation Task process. To gain clarity, they asked me to refer to your blog by sharing the link, which I found informative and appreciated your effort. However, after reviewing your videos, I still find some aspects unclear. If you don't mind, could you please look into these and provide clarification? Your insights would be greatly appreciated.

Question:

1) In your video, you directly navigate to the 'Manage Your Solution' app and land on the page where you can filter the "Application Area" and "Sub Application Area" for data processes, and find the "Configure" option under 'Define Balance Validation Task'. However, in my case, we don't reach this page directly. Instead, we see the message "The business configuration of this tenant is managed by SAP Central Business Configuration," which provides two links: one redirects to the SAP Help page, and the other to the SAP Central Business Configuration page. After selecting the relevant option, we still cannot find the filter option to configure the 'Define Balance Validation Task'. Instead, we see the "Configuration" option, which shows the same options as the Implementation page (Display IMG: Authorized Activities page). We are unable to locate the "Define Balance Validation Task". I have explained this issue in a ticket to the SAP Support Team as well.

2) According to KBA 3553668 and the SAP Help page, it is mentioned that "we need to toggle the feature for Balance Validation (FINCS_BV_ADAPTATION) in the configuration activity 'Activate Features.' However, we cannot find this in the system.

Page:https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/90c07e91c7a64f328be3fd6b48955b13/e01c5fc486f145be8f90f44c5f6b38d9.html

and in the SAP page they mentioned to "Activate Feature" which I was unable to find you followed this in the video ( I may consider this may automatically activated or backend enable to demonstrated this Video which I apricated) but I couldn't able to get the clarity on this and I was unable find in your video as well, Am I missing something to understand here?

3) I tried to drill down to get more information when i couldn't able find these option to enable it and If found the below page where SAP mentioned detailed about 'Activate Feature" 

SAP Page: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b249d650b15e4b3d9fc2077ee921abd0/3e94fcc37b42433b97d6aef0c830dc52.html

Regarding the mentioned in the "NOTE" - Do you have any inputs here, Im keen to understand how this process happen in your above "Demo" case and How it differs for customers. If you agree with the "Note", Do you have any time line when this will be activated to customers and will we receive any notification or communication regarding this?. Im unable to get clarity which they mentioned on the " At Certain Point". if you have any information Please share the same.

4) The following SAP page they mentioned to we can find the "Define task for line item validation" in configuration activity, But we are unable to find this in our system. I believe this may be related to the Pre-requisite above page and 'Activate feature' steps which I mentioned above.

URL:https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/998dafecbd6a41ef8a75c2b03a09eab7.html?parentHref=/whats-new/4328a3353fb14ef6ba3345697ac9ecc3?locale=en-US&parentName=Preliminary+What%27s+New+-+SAP+S/4HANA+Cloud+Public+Edition+2502

SAPURL: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b249d650b15e4b3d9fc2077ee921abd0/826e3120d0cd43d4a5cf75a93e07037d.html

 

5) SAP saying that it will deprecated Data Validation App in future release in the below page as part of 2502 upgrade.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/c0abeaddeb504c1c9365917e0c4e726c.html

Related to this, I have a question: will the deprecation of this app impact other apps like F8365, F2635, and F8364, which are indirectly linked to Data Monitor and Consolidation Monitor? I believe these apps serve as alternatives and can be navigated to individually. Once we run Data Monitor and Consolidation Monitor, the task log refers to the same pages of the above apps that are linked. I'm checking for any potential impact on this point?

6) With the introduction of new balance validation apps as part of the 2502 upgrade, will there be any additional rules delivered as part of the Best Practices content? Will the existing standard validation rules undergo any changes? I read on the SAP page that existing rules will be migrated. Will this happen gradually based on the note mentioned on the 'Activate Feature' page, or will they be automatically migrated once we toggle the 'Balance Validation Process' or enable it?

Thank you for your time

Srinivasa.S

Can we do something to have 'Search' also to consider the content in Comments?

Thanks,
Abhijit

SAP Community

entry.title: Crystal Reports - SAP Business One - GS1 Datamatrix Code
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

i like to create a Datamatrix Code with GS1 Standard. As far as I know Crystal report does not support Datamatrix Codes, but it can be achived with additional tools.

Who has live customers creating Datamatrix Codes (maybe even with GS1 Standard) and which tools are the using ?

 

Kind regards
Tobias

entry.title: Re: GR Print Preview in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi kaya-ya

Probably not directly from that app.

However, you will get a pop-up or result dialog after you do the post.

That dialog will list the Material Document created by the Post.

It is also a hyperlink - so if you click that link, you will be directed to the object page for the Material Document.

Then go to the item details

From there you can review the generated PDF.

This is of course you choose some output in the print input field during the post.

 

I hope this helps

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Ready to gain knowledge on AI techniques, including data masking, content filtering, and many more?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Using Advanced AI Techniques with SAP’s Generative AI Hub learning journey.

Overview
This journey builds upon foundational knowledge gained in the previous learning experience, "Solving Your Business Problems Using Prompts and LLMs in SAP's Generative AI Hub." By focusing on advanced AI techniques, including data masking, content filtering, document grounding, and vector embeddings, learners will deepen their understanding and expand the capabilities of their applications.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, participants will be able to effectively apply advanced AI techniques, including e.g. document grounding, and vector embeddings, to enhance business applications using SAP's Generative AI hub. They will have a comprehensive understanding of advanced AI techniques, the generative AI hub, and the practical implementation of these advanced techniques to solve complex business problems and improve operational efficiency.

Prerequisites

  • Solving Your Business Problems Using Prompts and LLMs in SAP's Generative AI Hub

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Entity Import & Model transfer for single query - SAP BW bridge
entry.content:
Hi Dominik,

Thank for sharing an informative blog. As i undertsand, we can migrate the BW query elements using SAP BW Bridge to SAP Datasphere. These elements will be available as entities.

1) This tool migrates one query at a time, most of the queries will have some common underlying inforproviders/infoobjects. How is this handled, will it create duplicate objects? 

2) Customer invested in BO reports on BW/4 HANA? Plans to migrate to SAP Datasphere. What is the optimal approach to remediate these BO reports in SAC? or do we need to recreate them which would be a lot of effort?

entry.title: Re: Sales Order Created via Client Certificate Authentica...
entry.content:
Hello,

When it comes to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, any document creation that occurs through automation and integration processes often falls under the Digital Access model. Digital Access accounting is based on the way documents are created in the system, rather than the authentication method used. Therefore, whether you use client certificate authentication, basic authentication, or OAuth2, the classification as Digital Access remains primarily dependent on how the document is generated.

**Is it expected that Sales Orders created via client certificate authentication are counted under Digital Access?**
Yes, it is expected that when Sales Orders are created via automated processes such as through SAP Business Process Automation using OData API, they are classified under the Digital Access model. This classification is not determined by the type of authentication but rather by the automated nature of the document creation.

**Would using a Named User with Basic Authentication or Oauth2 User token exchange authentication instead of a service key avoid the Digital Access classification?**
Switching the authentication method from a service key to a Named User with Basic Authentication or OAuth2 User token exchange will not avoid the classification under Digital Access. The key determining factor for Digital Access is the automation driving the document creation, not the type of authentication used.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: SAP TM - Tracking number update from carrier in Freight order in Basic SAP TM
entry.content:
We are doing a greenfield implementation of SAP S/4HANA 2023 where basic embedded SAP TM is being implemented.

The carriers on receiving the Freight order send an XML file back to SAP TM with tracking number.

<SOAP-ENV:Envelope
xmlns:SOAP-ENV="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/">
<SOAP-ENV:Header/>
<SOAP-ENV:Body>
<ProcessShipmentReply
xmlns="http://XXX.com/ws/ship/v26">
<HighestSeverity>SUCCESS</HighestSeverity>
<Notifications>
<Severity>SUCCESS</Severity>
<Source>ship</Source>
<Code>0000</Code>
<Message>Success</Message>
<LocalizedMessage>Success</LocalizedMessage>
</Notifications>
<TransactionDetail>
<CustomerTransactionId>1001022546</CustomerTransactionId>
</TransactionDetail>

I checked and found that SPROXY

TransportationOrderSCMRequest_In can be used to design the inbound XML file.

My problem is I cannot find a place in Freight order where this tracking id will get updated. SAP Event management is in Advanced TM which I cannot use.

I wanted to know how the whole inbound process of Tracking from carrier is configured in Basic TM.

Also checking the XML I believe I will have to do an enhancement in TransportationOrderSCMRequest_In

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
My ex-colleague has the same story!
Ciao e piacere 😄
Dove lavori? Come hai scoperto la community? 
Aggiungimi su LinkedIn se vuoi 😄

entry.title: Re: Difference between document currency amount and hard...
entry.content:
Hello

It is not necessarily same exchange rate. Check OB22 for exchange rate type or if you are in S/4HANA check FINSC_LEDGER under "Global Currency Conversion Settings" or "Currency Conversion Settings per Company Code." You might find different exhange rates for currency type 10 and 40.

entry.title: Re: How to include Project System in Make-to-Stock proces...
entry.content:
Hello @jatindra1986 

Please activate the Project Control - Sales (4I9) scope item and maintain the E2 (Engineer-to-Order Production) strategy group in the Material Master. Additionally, ensure that the relevant WBS element is assigned in the Sales Order line item Account Assignment tab.

So whenever an order is created for an MTO (Make-to-Order) product, the system will automatically determine the E21 (Proj, Sales W/O Plan) requirement type in the sales order.

Best Regards

Vivek Savant

entry.title: Re: MDK Binding and Control access issues
entry.content: This helped. Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Re: Date Difference Formula to use in XSL Statement
entry.content:
Hello @satnaik18 ,

This might work!

<xsl:template match="/dates"> <xsl:variable name="date1" select="xs:date(date1)" /> <xsl:variable name="date2" select="xs:date(date2)" /> <!-- Calculate the difference in days --> <xsl:variable name="date-diff" select="xs:date($date2) - xs:date($date1)" /> <!-- Output the difference --> <difference> <xsl:value-of select="$date-diff" /> </difference> </xsl:template>

Try adding code in a notepad++ to view in actual format and understand the code. You can probably use chatgpt as well. Try doing a preview in some testing sites like https://www.w3schools.com/xml/xml_xslt.asp

Good Luck!

Regards,

Surya

entry.title: Re: Best Practice for Productive Indicator During Asset M...
entry.content: The company code can be set to productive in OBY6 but in asset accounting (FAA_CMP tode in S/4HANA) it must be set up "For Testing" until all conversion tasks are completed (customizing conversion as well as data migration).

entry.title: Re: MRP relevant for scheduling agreement
entry.content:
Hello Johannes ,

May I know how cumulative quantities works for sa in suppliers how does the system calculates and make zero end of the year .

entry.title: SAP CDS View Entity - Joining Table View
entry.content:
Hello *,
I am currently exploring the possibilities of SAP CDS View Entities and trying to replace various SQL statements in our coding with CDS View Entities to improve performance. In doing so, I have encountered several situations where the Open SQL statement (which we are using now) performs JOINs on table views (for example V_EQUI).

So far, I have not been able to integrate a JOIN on the view V_EQUI into a CDS View Entity. Is this even possible? If so, how can this be achieved?

Thank you in advance!

Best Regards

Marius

ABAP DevelopmentSAP HANA

entry.title: GR activity is falling on weekend
entry.content: GR activity of production order is falling on weekend because of 7 days plant factory calendar. Requirement is that GR activity should not happen on weekend without changing the plant factory calendar to 5 days. In PPDS this is possible by assigning a 5 days calendar to handling resource but in S/4 side it refers to plant factory calendar and material is available on weekend. The 7 days plant calendar has been set to enable Ad-hoc order execution on weekend.

entry.title: Re: Here we go: The Terraform provider for SAP BTP release 1.0.0 is available
entry.content:
Hi @lechnerc77 ,

Just Curious, Could you please share if the recent acquisition of HashiCorp by IBM has any impact on the usage of Terraform in the SAP BTP platform?

https://newsroom.ibm.com/2025-02-27-ibm-completes-acquisition-of-hashicorp,-creates-comprehensive,-end-to-end-hybrid-cloud-platform

Thanks 

Sree

entry.title: Re: Difference between document currency amount and hard...
entry.content:
Hello
Thank you for the response
I checked in OB22 where different exchange rate types are maintained for company code currency and hard currency 
Co code currency (type 10) USD - Ex rate type M
Hard currency (type 40) - Ex rate type EPCC (Exploration production concession rate)

entry.title: SAP TM - Drop shipment and returns scenario for Transport process
entry.content:
We are doing greenfield SAP S/4HANA 2023implementation where Basic embedded TM is implemented.

For a normal Order to cash scenario SO- Delivery- FU-FO-FS flow is implemented.

The issue is coming with drop shipment scenario and returns.

In drop shipment business is saying they take care of transportation from vendor to customer. Since the flow does not contain any delivery, I am not able to activate creation of Freight unit from delivery. This is basic TM so creation of FU from sales order or Purchase order is not possible. How do I configure the scenario? Do I have to create dummy delivery type?

In return process business says they do the transportation from customer to warehouse but sometimes the customer does the transportation. If I activate the return delivery for FU creation, it will create FU in both cases. How do I distinguish or create a flow where customer does the transportation? Even If I leave the FU without creating FO in case of customer doing the transportation, How do I close the FU?

entry.title: Re: Considering Implementing Dynamic Offer Letters in SuccessFactors? Here is Our Story
entry.content:
Hello @Agustina_Palma 

Thank you for your comment! We did experience formatting issues with the offer letters as well. They did create some additional spacing issues within the letters and it did create some re-work for the team to go through and clean up. I would recommend in utilizing the source button in Offer Letters to view the  HTML and go through in editing the letter.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Kit to Stock- Post Kit not visible
entry.content: @Yuki_Hino1

entry.title: Re: SAP ABAP JSON to ABAP Structure
entry.content:
Hi,

There are not enough details to understand what the issue is, but looking into the JSON, I assume it may be connected to how to deserialize the "form" node. It would be problematic to get it in using deserialize into REF TO data or GENERATE (because of not mappable attribute names). Which is any case not recommended for different reasons (see FAQ section of the GitHub page mentioned by Sandra Rossi).

So my suggestion will be here to deserialize into a fixed output structure and for the "form" field use a sorted table with ASSOC_ARRAY + ASSOC_ARRAY_OPT flags. This shall work and will bring the best results.

BR, Alexey.

entry.title: MaxDB Dababase Studion 7.9.11.08 Instalation
entry.content:
Hi,

I tried to update the MaxDB DatabaseStudion from 7.9.10.11 to 7.9.11.08.
During installation I geht the message.
“cannot extract sapjvm/sapjvm_8_jre/bin/npt.dll: cannot open C:\Prgram Files\sdb\DatabaseStudion/sapjvm/sapjvm_8_jre/bin/npt.dll for writing: invalid argument”

Wenn starting Database Studio I get this message. “Error: could not open ‘C:\Program Files\sdb\DatabaseStudion\sapjvm\sapjvm_8_jre\lib\amd64\jvm.cfg”

I have a Windows 11 machine.
I have already tried to uninstall the DatabaseStudion and install it new same result.
I have added pictures of the messages.
What do I have to do to get the DatabaseStudion to work?

Thanks and BR
HP

entry.title: Re: Here we go: The Terraform provider for SAP BTP release 1.0.0 is available
entry.content:
@sm46 the finalization of the acquisition of Hashicorp by IBM has as of today no impact on the providers offered by us. With the current setup at IBM  we will continue to support Hashicorp Terraform and OpenTofu with our providers.

We will of course watch how things evolve in this space if there might be changes that force us to re-evaluate this decision.

entry.title: Re: Here we go: The Terraform provider for SAP BTP release 1.0.0 is available
entry.content: got it, thanks for your reply.

entry.title: Filter by fixed date range does not allow selection unless I edit the filter every month
entry.content:
1) I understand the values in the dataset drive the range of available values...

2) editing the date range every month is a bit annoying...

At the beginning of each month, I must go in and edit the date range filter on my story to include that month.  The date range filter is set to FIXED, because the DYNAMIC filter does not provide the SLIDER.

Dynamic

Any suggestions?

James

entry.title: ERROR: Enter a warehouse process type/SCWM/L35
entry.content:
Scenario - Planned replenishment with POSC LOSC steps. 

Configured POSC with two steps - step 1 move replenishment quantity to interim repacking area, Step 2 move the quantity to final replenishment storage bin

combined with LOSC - to move replenishment quantity from interim repack area to level 2 storage area, and then move from level 2 storage area to final replenishment storage bin. 

First WT got triggered from the picking storage type to interim packing area as per the POSC setup after running planned replenishment using t-code /SCWM/REPL.

After HU is completed at the interim packing area work center - next WT creation as per LOSC is failing due to the following error

"Enter a warehouse process type    /SCWM/L3    5"

WPT is configures as part of POSC/LOSC setup but still system isn"t able to pick it up.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content: Hello! Wishing you the best in your SAP endeavors. Please join Interest Groups | SAP Community where you are interested to engage!

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content: @okedigwe nice to meet you. Be sure to join Interest Groups | SAP Community to get engaged and meet community members. Best of luck!

entry.title: Re: Important Update: Changes to the Free Tier Option for SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content:
@foseb14

The recommended contractual types for SAP BTP ABAP Environment are CPEA/BTPEA and Pay-as-you-go. These contractual types are "consumption-based". In contrast to subscriptions, only the actual usage is settled on hourly basis according to the actional existence of ABAP systems. It means, if you delete a system, no costs will be charged (except the upfront payment for CPEA/BTPEA).

You can find more information here: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/serviceCatalog/abap-environment?tab=service_plan&region=all and here: https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/pricing.html

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: @Mohammed_Yaseen_SAC be sure to join Interest Groups | SAP Community to engage with other members. Good luck!

entry.title: Payment Wizard didn't generate any documents
entry.content:
Hello Community,

My client is having a problem with the Payment Wizard where he executed the payment wizard and it didn´t generate any document, didn't also generate any error from step 7 to step 8.

After selecting the option "Execute Payment Run" the Summary says that were created a total of 0 documents.

My client selected the respective BP and also selected the documents which he wants the outgoing payments to be created.

*Images Attached

Can someone help please?

entry.title: Re: Important Update: Changes to the Free Tier Option for SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content:
@NoelJohnson 

The amount of the released objects is the same per release - independent of shared trial, free tier or commercial offerings. 

If a function module is not offered - there might be 3 reasons:

  • SAP BTP ABAP Environment provides only the technology stack and application basis. It does *not* contain S/4HANA objects from e.g. Financials etc.
    You can simply search in ADT via ctrl+A this function module name. If the fm does not appear - it is not part in the software stack 
  • If the function module appears, it contains either to a non-released component or the functionality of the function module is now offered by a class-based API. In that case, open the Release State tab in the properties view in ADT. There is a section successor which might point to the released class-based API

There is a released CDS to introspect all released objects.

You can find an overview about all released components here: https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-btp-abap-environment/abap-environment/released-components-and-objects

entry.title: Integrating SAP Fiori screens with external UI
entry.content: Hello, we have a custom developed UI with custom screens that is currently being used as a substitute for SAP GUI screens as per biz requirement. There are API's that we have developed that assure data flow from the UI system to SAP and vice versa. Now to this, I just wanted to know the feasibility/possibility of hosting SAP fiori screens to the custom UI that we had developed. Is this in anyway possible to integrate SAP Fiori custom screens to an external UI system that already has a data flow in place?

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Learning best practices for file namin...
entry.content: @Teresa_W, trying to contribute, if you'd like to review the auto-generated IDs for certain entities, here you can find additional details: Forcing System Generated IDs.

entry.title: SAP Concur user authentication to concursolutions.com
entry.content:
I have been a Technical Consultant at SAP Concur for many years and I understand how important security is in our digital world today. Security has always been a crucial element throughout the entire product lifecycle of all SAP products, including product development, planning, and quality assurance.

In this post, I will explain the three authentication methods that can be used by end users to log in to concursolutions.com.

Introduction

 

Security aspects that may require business decisions are managed by the customers of SAP Concur. A typical example of this is user and authorization management.

There are three different methods that users of SAP Concur can use to authenticate. In this blog post, I will provide a summary of the three different authentication methods, which can be defined as follows:

  1. Sign in with SAP Concur password
  2. Sign in with Email link (Passwordless Sign-in)
  3. Sign in with Single Sign-on

 

Detailed Walkthrough

 

Sign in with SAP Concur password 

This method of authentication is represented by logging in on concursolutions.com via the username and password that are stored in SAP Concur.The password authentication is one of the most commonly used login methods. Therefore, it is important to educate users about the risks associated with weak passwords.
Users of SAP Concur can sign in to their personal profiles by using the password stored within SAP Concur. There are few points that I strongly recommend:

  • Do not share your password with anyone. SAP Concur Support will never ask you to disclose your profile password.
  • Use a unique and complex password for each account to prevent unauthorized access if one gets compromised.
  • Be vigilant of phishing email messages that asks you to click on links of provide sensitive information. For more information, refer to the Phishing topic.

The user with the company administrator role for your SAP Concur system can define the password policy on the Sign-In Settings page.


Accessing Sign-In Settings

To access the Sign-In Settings page the company administrator clicks on the Home button -> Authentication Admin.

On the Authentication Administration page, click Sign-In Settings.


This page allows administrators to configure the following requirements and parameters:

  • Password strength - There you can define the minimum length of passwords that users must use. Nowadays, passwords with 12 to 14 characters, including uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, and special characters such as #, %, and @, are considered safe.

 

  • Password change - In this section, you can set how long a user can use the same password and how often a user can change their password. The password expiration feature must be strictly applied to align with the 90-day password expiration policy recommended by SAP Concur.

 

  • Account Lockout - This is a feature that specifies after how many failed password attempts, within a certain timeframe, the user will be locked out. The lockout will persist for a specified period or until the password is reset by either the user or the administrator if it is set to 'permanent lockout'. This feature provides a robust defense against brute force techniques, which rely on guessing the correct password.

 

  • Session Timeout - This feature defines the idle time before automatic user sign-out from SAP Concur. For example, if you log in to concursolutions.com to perform an action and then go for lunch while still having the SAP Concur session open in your browser, it will enter an idle state. After 30 minutes of inactivity, while you are having lunch, you will be logged out from the website.

 

  • Other Settings - Here you will find some additional policy settings. You can restrict the username from being sent to the user's email if it is forgotten. Additionally, you can require the user to change their initial password upon their first login.

The third option is related to the Two-Factor Authentication and the requirement for the user to receive an activation email link. Enabling this option is strongly recommended, as it adds an extra layer of security to user accounts in your SAP Concur system.

The fourth option involves the enablement of the passwordless sign-in feature, which will be covered in the section below: 'Sign in with Email Link (Passwordless Sign-in).' 

 

The authentication method to Sign in with SAP Concur password requires a two-factor authentication (2FA) to be setup and used.

When an SAP Concur user signs in using their username and password for the first time, they will be prompted to set up 2FA. During the setup process, the user will receive an email from SAP Concur with further instructions on how to configure 2FA.

The user must then use a third-party authenticator mobile application to scan a QR code or manually enter a key during the 2FA enrollment process.

Once 2FA is set up, users signing in to SAP Concur with a username and password must use 2FA during the sign-in process.

This means users will be prompted to enter a one-time 6-digit code generated by an authenticator mobile application each time they sign in to SAP Concur. For more information on the 2FA feature, you can read more here. - https://dam.sap.com/mac/app/p/pdf/asset/preview/FrhUmfQ?ltr=a&rc=10

 

Sign in with Email link (Passwordless Sign-in)

This feature is available only for the web-based version of SAP Concur. It is not available for the mobile-based login or the mobile application of SAP Concur.

By using it, the user can receive an email with a one-time link to sign in. The user should have a valid email address configured in their profile. This method of authentication provides a smoother and more efficient user experience without compromising security.

The one-time link has an expiry time of 1 hour and is sent only to the user.

The security aspect relies on the user's email account, which needs to be well-maintained and secured to prevent compromise. Passwordless sign-in is enabled by default for clients who have Single Sign-On set as optional. Company administrators can enable or disable the feature in the Sign-In Settings.

More about this feature can be found here - https://dam.sap.com/mac/app/p/pdf/asset/preview/z7oNDZn?ltr=a&rc=10&doi=SAP1114633

 

 Sign in with Single Sign-on

 The third option for user authentication that SAP Concur supports is Single Sign-On (SSO) via the SAML 2.0 standard.

SAML SSO functionality involves two parties: an Identity Provider (IdP) and a Service Provider (SP). In this situation, SAP Concur is the service provider.

SAP Concur supports any identity providers that comply with the SAML 2.0 standard. SAP Concur supports both IdP-Initiated and SP-Initiated SSO.

The IdP-Initiated SSO is explained with the following scenario: users sign in to the identity provider and then click on a link or tile on the IdP page to access SAP Concur.

With SP-Initiated SSO, the user navigates to concursolutions.com, enters their username, and selects the appropriate SSO option. After users successfully authenticate with the identity provider, an active session of SAP Concur will be opened. The SP-Initiated authentication flow is used by the mobile application of SAP Concur.

The SAP Concur user that has the permission called 'SSO admin' will have access to the Single Sign-On Self Service tool, where customer can manage their own SSO configuration.

From the SSO self-service tool the SSO administrators can obtain the SAP Concur SP metadata that needs to be uploaded into the customer's identity provider. After that the IdP metadata file needs to be generated from the customer's identity provider and uploaded to SAP Concur.

To access the SSO self-service tool the company administrator clicks on the Home button -> Authentication Admin. On the Authentication Administration page, click on Manage Single Sign-on.

The SSO admin of the company can set the SSO sign-in policy on the Manage Single Sign-on page.

 

By default, the SSO sign-in policy is set to "SSO Optional." This setting ensures that all three different methods of authentication in SAP Concur are enabled: "Sign in with SAP Concur password," "Sign in with email link (passwordless sign-in)," and "Sign in with single sign-on." 

"If the SSO sign-in policy is changed to 'SSO Required,' all users will need to sign in to SAP Concur through an identity provider using SSO."

Be cautious when enabling the SSO Required policy because all users in your SAP Concur system—including TMCs, administrators, web services, and test user accounts—will be blocked from signing into concursolutions.com with their username and password stored in SAP Concur.

Currently, the SSO sign-in policy can only be applied at the company level.

In the ‘Manage Single Sign-On’ page, customers can upload an unlimited number of IdP metadata files. This allows customers to connect an unlimited number of IdP applications to a single SAP Concur entity. The SSO administrators can assign different names to each configured IdP, such as ‘Okta (Company A).’

This helps users know which one to choose during SP-initiated SSO sign-in.

 

While configuring an IdP in Managed Single Sign-On, the SSO administrators can choose to hide the SSO option from the login page of SAP Concur. Enabling this setting is suitable if you are performing SSO testing.

 

The SSO admin can specify a logout URL that will redirect the user to a specific page after signing out of SAP Concur.

Here, you will find more resources about the SSO setup in SAP Concur - https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CONCUR_SECURITY/b92b8c7fc75a4c8faf62a6584077b022/8bfaad63987d455183bf7cdcd75c578a.html

Conclusion


After reading this post, you will know more about the three authentication methods for accessing concursolutions.com and be able to choose the right combination for your needs.

 

Share and Connect


What do you think?  Do you have anything to add? Leave a comment below.

Did you find it useful? Give us a like and share on social media.

Want to know more about SAP Concur? Please follow here.

Want to ask questions about SAP Concur and its offerings? Ask here

Follow my profile for similar content.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Integration of CIM with S/4 Hana Cloud
entry.content: Read this:https://help.sap.com/docs/CENTRAL_INVOICE_MANAGEMENT/eed6f62fbe144d7f87d7397d673a2ffe/047aabe01dbb406dba28cd18969ba092.html

entry.title: Re: Integration of CIM with S/4 Hana Cloud
entry.content: The scheduler imports most, but not all, of the Company Code information needed by SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management. To import the information that's not supported by the scheduler, you use the Import Master Data from File app after using the scheduler.

entry.title: Re: Managing selection in tnt:NavigationList?
entry.content: Hi all,

entry.title: Re: Managing selection in tnt:NavigationList?
entry.content: Hi All, for me worked this.getView().byId('idNavigationList').mProperties.selectedKey = null;

entry.title: Re: How to download/extract custom Fiori app source code...
entry.content: Unfortunately when I try this I get the following error: "Transaction /UI5/UI5_REPOSITORY_ does not exist"

entry.title: SAP TM ERP Document Flow Navigation Through Fiori
entry.content:
SAP TM ERP Document Flow Navigation Through Fiori

SAP Transportation Management (TM) allow users to navigate form the ERP documents direct to the Freights Documents.

In the previous version of SAP TM, the navigation through the Freight Documents was in NWBC, which was the Front End for the Key User, now with S/4HANA Key User use the Fiori Launchpad.

Through the navigation from the ERP documents Key Users can see many data and follow up the process of Planning and execution for the Sales Order, Delivery, etc.

For example, from a Sales Order user goes to TM Status Tab and see a lot of information from the Freight Documents.

TM Document Flow.

Every Use who has access to navigate through the TM Document flow jus clicks on the Freight Document, and it open a new session in the browser to visualize the Freight Document.

If you notice in the Browser once you open i.e. a Freight Unit the section is based on NWBC, this means you can still do any action if need in the Freight Unit but you’re not able to navigation through the Fiori Launchpad.

From a Key Users perspective this session of the Freight Unit is exclusive and can’t get back to the Fiori Launchpad

From a technical point of view if you want to allow the Key Users navigate to the Fiori Launchpad and not NWBC, you can add the User parameter WEBGUI_IS_IN_FLP = Y, in Transaction SU3 this will allow the Key Users open the Freights Documents from the ERP direct in the the Fiori Launchpad mode.

After adding this parameter, you can see the difference for the Fiori Launchpad mode.

Note: Login in the Fiori Launchpad might be request.

Refer to;

3577612 - How to navigate to the Fiori Launchpad to open TM documents from ERP document flow. - SAP for Me

3199167 - TM: Create fully-qualified FLP-based document URLs - SAP for Me

2708480 - SAP TM embedded in S/4HANA - PFCG role concept - SAP for Me

 

Hope this help you!

Thank you and regards.

Rogerio Lima.

entry.title: Re: EC Time and Attendance - new features Q4 2018
entry.content:
Hi @Volker_Ruof ,

I understand the reasoning for having the break deducted on the absence day. However, this is creating a short time of 30 minutes in my working time account. How can I prevent this?

Thanks,

Manon

entry.title: Re: Problematic "Unnamed Object" in Fiori Elements app po...
entry.content:
Two possible causes come to mind, but I am just speculating here since I haven't tested how this would behave.

  • The table underlying the data you have exposed is not defined with the "Inital Values" flag. Therefore your data have NULL values
  • Your data model have defined these as foreign keys, and the foreign key check table (e.g. T001W for your Plant field) doesn't have an entry with the initial value

Remember: The more information you provide, the better answers you get. You have provided very little information here, so the quality of the answers reflect that.

entry.title: Re: Credly certificate
entry.content:
Hi @Lillian_888 
Please share some more details e.g. the name and the URL of the BTM3 you are referring to. 
So I can analyze the case.
Kind regards 
Margit

entry.title: How to activate your S-User ID
entry.content:
Log in to SAP for Me and start exploring everything the platform has to offer today! 

                                     

A company that has a support contract with SAP and a Super Administrator set up within the company can create your S-User ID. You’ll receive an automatically generated welcome e-mail. This email will allow you to activate your S-User which can then be linked to your Universal ID.   

For whatever reason your S-User activation may have expired or perhaps you accidentally deleted the activation e-mail.  

Here are the steps to follow for an alternative activation method:

  1. Go to Forgot My Password.  

  1. Enter your S-user ID and click continue.   
  2. Check your inbox for an e-mail sent from SAP ID Service (ias@notifications.sap.com) to your S-user e-mail address.  
  3. Open the e-mail and click on the password reset link.  

 To note:   

  • The password reset link is valid for 2 hours from the time that it was generated from the system and can only be used once. If the password link expires, repeat the steps 1–4.   
  • You can only change your password once in a 24-hour period. 

  Now your S-User has been activated it will be available to add to your Universal ID! 

 Check out this blog to add your newly activated to your Universal ID: SAP Universal ID: Tips and Tricks - SAP Community    

For more information on S-User ID’s take a look at this blog: Learn All About S-User IDs! - SAP Community  

 Troubleshooting Tips:   

If you haven’t received the password reset e-mail after a few minutes:  

  • Check your SPAM/junk folder.  
  • Clear your browsing history, cookies, and cache and repeat the steps. 
  • Check that your company email settings are not blocking emails from SAP.  

 If you still haven't received the e-mail: Contact the SAP ID Service Team (SSO Team) at the following email address: sso@sap.com. You'll see more details on the SSO Team here: When and How to contact the SAP ID Service Team

                                                                                                                                                       

Thanks for reading!   

We hope you found this useful. Please note that the Customer Interaction Center is always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have. 

If you have any questions, please ask a questions in the Q & A using All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community

Read more ##CustomerInteractionCenter blog-posts here!

entry.title: Re: Opening transaction in SAPGUI for HTML via URL fails...
entry.content:
I am now using a direct link to the WebGUI:

https://<host>/sap/bc/gui/sap/its/webgui?
~transaction=*QM02%20RIWO00-QMNUM=000200001234?sap-client=<client>

It looks like this approach is much more reliable than using the FIORI environment.

entry.content:

Do you think it's possible?

Regards

it could make sense to maintain on the same target group /PRODUCTION/ either PS4 and RS4 to use "transport checks" ?

entry.title: AW01N - values for ordinary depreciation have changed in depreciation area 02 and 32
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We went live on January 2025 for our India local company code that belongs to Group company located in USA. So typically we have set-up two FYV i.e. 0L- Jan to Dec and L1- Apr to March. But in S/4 2021 on prem in-order to use AA module your FYV periods should same further we have created additional ledger with R1 and assign same FYV (Jan to Dec) and done the required system set-up.

We followed year-end approach where-in the transfer date was 12.31.2024 (FAA_CMP) in which Highest FY is 2025 and Closed FY is 2024 for 0L and R1 ledger. Now, currently what we have observe after AA legacy data load of APC and Accumulated depreciation values through migration cockpit is whenever we are executing AW01N report system is showing message 'values for ordinary depreciation have changed in depreciation area 02 and 32' (India local-GAAP depreciation areas) SAP message AU390. Post which system is looking for AFAR not sure why? And not sure what went wrong and how to proceed further. Please note: Till now we haven't run any depreciation for the new company code

Best Regards,

Gourav Bose

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Unable to finf RFC destination for QM connection
entry.content: Link not found ERROR 404

entry.title: Re: Unable to finf RFC destination for QM connection
entry.content: Link is invalid.

entry.title: Two documents are saved instead of one
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I know that in principle BTE is not intended for validations, but in our system, there are validations located in BTE2218. Now we've noticed that in the following scenario, two documents are being saved, even though the user entered only one document. (Two records are inserted into BKPF). The scenario:

  • The user enters invoice data in FV60 and saves it (as parked).
  • Code within BTE2218 performs a check, and as a result:
    • A message is displayed (even if no message, the process is incorrect).
    • The process is broken by a leave screen.
  • The user corrects the data on the screen and saves it again (as parked).
  • The system saves two documents, not one. (Two records are inserted into BKPF).

From the debugging, it seems that with every save attempt, the system creates a new record (with a new document number). When the second save attempt is valid and a commit work is executed, both records are saved in the database, even though one record is invalid.

In the debug, it appears that the internal table for BKPF contains only one record each time. My assumption is that there is an internal table that gathers the two records, and in the end, the update to BKPF is done according to this table. I couldn't find this internal table or the place where the DB update is done .

How can this be fixed?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAC - Uploading Dimension with Append
entry.content:
Hi,

you can schedule it every day as per the loads observation and also check the Import settings.

entry.title: Re: Create Custom Mass Activity for your Program - Config
entry.content: Could you let us know how you created the CUSTOM parallelization object ? as it is checking for entry in table TFKDI

entry.title: Setup and usage of contract accounts
entry.content:
Hello,
We are reviewing our FI-CA administration and want to include more SD flows into FI-CA.
I was wondering if there are people in this community who can share their knowledge regarding the definition of contract account types and usage of contract accounts.
So for example: should we use one contract account type and create multiple contract accounts, or is it a best practice to define several contract account types.

We already have 2 divisions (electricity and gas) in use to support our IS-U processes. I expect some additional divisions to be created, because not all our products/services will fit into one of those divisions.

Best Regards,
Henk Hoogerbrug

entry.title: Re: SAP CDS View Entity - Joining Table View
entry.content: A little search gives the answer immediately: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/part-8-cds-views-joins-and-associations/ba-p/13406513
